text:
CT ABDOMEN AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
MAINTENANCE RESTAGING EXAMINATION

CLINICAL INDICATION: 58-year-old male with known Stage IV KRAS G12D-mutant sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma with bilobar hepatic metastases. Status post 12 cycles of mFOLFOX6 plus bevacizumab (induction), now approximately 6-8 weeks into maintenance LV5FU2 plus bevacizumab. Prior imaging demonstrated partial response after induction with residual hepatic metastases and primary sigmoid mass. This examination performed for routine surveillance on maintenance regimen. Comparison: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis from approximately 8 weeks prior (end-of-induction restaging).

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the abdomen and pelvis acquired in the portal venous phase following administration of 100 mL of iohexol intravenous contrast. Coronal and sagittal reformats reviewed. No oral contrast administered per department protocol for oncologic surveillance. Images reviewed on dedicated PACS workstation with multiplanar reconstructions.

---

FINDINGS:

LIVER AND BILIARY SYSTEM:
The liver is normal in size and morphology. The hepatic parenchyma enhances homogeneously in the background. Four known hepatic metastatic lesions are again identified, all showing peripheral rim enhancement with central hypodensity consistent with treated/necrotic metastases:

- Segment VI lesion: measures 1.5 x 1.2 cm in axial dimension (previously 1.6 x 1.3 cm at end-of-induction scan). This lesion remains well-defined, hypodense on portal venous phase, and shows no significant interval change. Internal architecture is heterogeneous with a central low-density region, consistent with treatment-related necrosis. No new peripheral nodularity or satellite nodules identified.

- Segment II lesion: measures 0.6 x 0.5 cm (unchanged from prior). This lesion lies in close proximity to the left hepatic vein, as noted on prior surgical planning review. It remains small and difficult to characterize fully due to size, but shows the same morphology as on the preceding examination - a faint hypodense focus at the expected location.

- Segment IV lesion: measures 0.7 x 0.5 cm (unchanged from prior; previously 0.8 x 0.6 cm). This lesion demonstrates similar low-density characteristics to the segment II lesion and is stable in size.

- Segment VII lesion: not visualized on this examination, consistent with prior assessment of near-complete or complete response of this lesion. No recurrent hypodense focus or enhancing nodule is seen in this region.

No new hepatic lesions are identified. No biliary ductal dilatation. The gallbladder is normal, without stones or wall thickening. Portal vein is patent with normal hepatopetal flow characteristics on portal venous phase imaging.

SPLEEN:
Normal in size at approximately 11.2 cm in craniocaudal dimension. No focal splenic lesions. No splenomegaly.

PANCREAS:
The pancreas is normal in caliber throughout its head, neck, body, and tail without ductal dilatation or focal pancreatic mass. No peripancreatic lymphadenopathy.

ADRENAL GLANDS:
Bilateral adrenal glands are normal in morphology and size. No adrenal nodules or masses identified.

KIDNEYS AND URETERS:
Bilateral kidneys are normal in size with symmetric cortical enhancement and no hydronephrosis. No renal masses or renal calculi identified. Ureters are not dilated.

GASTROINTESTINAL TRACT:
Primary sigmoid colon tumor: An area of focal wall thickening and soft tissue mass in the sigmoid colon measures approximately 2.3 x 1.8 cm in its greatest cross-sectional dimensions (previously 2.4 x 1.9 cm at end-of-induction restaging). The appearance is essentially unchanged. There is mild pericolonic fat haziness/stranding in the immediate pericolic fat, consistent with the known locally invasive primary tumor and prior treatment effect. No new sites of colonic obstruction. The remaining large bowel is not dilated. The appendix appears normal.

Small bowel loops are unremarkable without evidence of obstruction, wall thickening, or mesenteric infiltration.

PERITONEUM AND MESENTERY:
No ascites. No peritoneal nodularity or omental caking to suggest peritoneal carcinomatosis. No free air. No pathologically enlarged peritoneal implants identified. Mesenteric fat shows no new inflammatory stranding or nodularity beyond the known pericolic changes at the primary tumor site.

LYMPH NODES:
No pathologically enlarged retroperitoneal, mesenteric, or pelvic lymph nodes. Previously noted pericolonic lymph nodes in the sigmoid mesentery are not discretely identifiable as separate from the primary mass and surrounding treated tissues; no clearly enlarged regional nodal disease. No new lymphadenopathy.

VASCULATURE:
The abdominal aorta is of normal caliber without aneurysmal dilatation. Celiac axis, superior mesenteric artery, and inferior mesenteric artery origins appear patent without occlusion. Inferior vena cava is patent.

PELVIS:
Bladder is well-distended and normal in wall thickness. No pelvic sidewall or iliac nodal enlargement. No pelvic free fluid. Prostate is within normal size limits for patient age.

MUSCULOSKELETAL / OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:
Visualized osseous structures, including the lumbar spine, sacrum, pelvis, and proximal femora, appear unremarkable without focal lytic or sclerotic lesions to suggest osseous metastatic involvement.

SOFT TISSUES:
No subcutaneous or soft tissue masses. Body habitus is consistent with prior examinations.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Stable hepatic metastatic disease compared to the most recent prior examination (end-of-induction CT approximately 8 weeks ago). The three measurable hepatic lesions in segments II, IV, and VI demonstrate no meaningful interval change in size or morphology and continue to reflect treatment-related necrosis/response. The segment VII lesion remains imperceptible, consistent with prior near-complete or complete response of that deposit.

2. Sigmoid colon primary tumor is essentially unchanged in size and appearance, without evidence of new local complications such as perforation, fistula, or complete luminal obstruction.

3. No new hepatic lesions, no peritoneal disease, no new intra-abdominal lymphadenopathy, and no osseous or adrenal metastases identified on this examination.

4. Chest not included in this examination. Correlation with chest imaging as clinically indicated to complete surveillance of known and potential pulmonary sites.

5. Overall pattern is consistent with maintained disease control on current maintenance chemotherapy regimen. No new sites of disease are demonstrated within the imaged field.

Radiologist: [Attending Radiologist signature on file]
Review: [Attending Radiologist - Body Imaging]
Transcriptionist: [Dictation service]
Copies to: Medical Oncology, Hepatobiliary Surgery
